fields.System.Title: Implement Case Draft edit with eligibility validation
Testing 183760
fields.System.Description:
Member Search — reduced test plan

1. UI compliance

  1. Verify Member Search section is displayed on the "Start a Prior Authorization" page

  2. Verify search fields Insurance Provider, Date of Birth, Member ID, First Name, and Last Name are displayed

  3. Verify required fields Insurance Provider and Date of Birth are marked with an asterisk

  4. Verify identifying set instruction and OR separator between Member ID and Name fields are displayed

2. Field validation

  1. Verify search cannot be executed when required fields (Insurance Provider and Date of Birth) are not provided

  2. Verify Date of Birth validation rules (valid date, not in the future, not earlier than 01/01/1900)

  3. Verify Member ID validation rules (whitespace trimming, max length, payer pattern validation)

  4. Verify First Name and Last Name validation rules (whitespace trimming, minimum length ≥ 3, allowed characters)

3. Positive search combinations

  1. Verify member search using Insurance Provider, Date of Birth, and Member ID

  2. Verify member search using Insurance Provider, Date of Birth, First Name, and Last Name

  3. Verify member search using Insurance Provider, Date of Birth, Member ID, First Name, and Last Name

4. Functional behavior

  1. Verify search returns the member when exactly one matching member is found

  2. Verify search is executed only within the selected Health Plan

  3. Verify user can switch between Member ID search path and Name search path

5. Edge cases

  1. Verify search returns Not Found when Member ID and Name belong to different members

  2. Verify search returns Not Found when no member matches the search criteria

  3. Verify search returns Not Found when more than one distinct member matches the search criteria

  4. Verify search returns the member when multiple eligibility spans exist for the same member


Open questions for BA

  1. What is the configured maximum length for Member ID for the payer used in testing?

  2. What is the configured Member ID pattern / expression for the payer used in testing?

  3. What exact validation message should be displayed for invalid Member ID?

  4. What is the maximum length for First Name and Last Name?

  5. Should name matching be case-sensitive or case-insensitive?

  6. What exact message should be shown for Not Found scenarios?

  7. What exact message should be shown when multiple members match?

fields.System.Title: Testing 183762
fields.System.Description:
Checklist — View/Edit Member Information Form for Found Member

UI / rendering

  1. Verify Member Information form is displayed after a successful member search result

  2. Verify the Member Information form displays the expected logical sections

    • Demographic Information

    • Member Contact Information

    • Member Representative Information

    • Eligibility Information

    • Treatment Information

  3. Verify eligibility-derived member information fields are rendered as read-only

  4. Verify Treatment Start Date is rendered as editable and empty by default

  5. Verify navigation actions are displayed on the Member Information form

Data display

  1. Verify demographic and eligibility information is populated from eligibility data

  2. Verify member contact and representative information is displayed when available

  3. Verify member information is displayed as read-only without allowing correction or override of eligibility-derived data

Validation / functional behavior

  1. Verify Treatment Start Date accepts a valid date and applies validation rules before proceeding

  2. Verify the user can proceed to the next step when required member information is available

  3. Verify displaying the Member Information form does not create, persist, or update a case record

Edge cases

  1. Verify optional eligibility attributes are hidden or displayed blank when they are missing

  2. Verify progression to the next step is blocked when required eligibility attributes are missing

  3. Verify the representative section is hidden or displayed empty when member representative information is not available

  4. Verify displayed member information is not automatically refreshed when eligibility data changes after the form is rendered

  5. Verify no case record is created when the user leaves the Member Information form without saving

  6. Verify user attempts to edit eligibility-derived member information fields are blocked

Shorter version if you want very compact coverage

  1. Verify Member Information form is displayed after a successful member search result

  2. Verify the Member Information form displays the expected logical sections

  3. Verify eligibility-derived member information fields are rendered as read-only

  4. Verify Treatment Start Date is rendered as editable and empty by default

  5. Verify demographic and eligibility information is populated from eligibility data

  6. Verify member contact and representative information is displayed when available

  7. Verify Treatment Start Date accepts a valid date and applies validation rules before proceeding

  8. Verify the user can proceed to the next step when required member information is available

  9. Verify displaying the Member Information form does not create, persist, or update a case record

  10. Verify optional eligibility attributes are hidden or displayed blank when they are missing

  11. Verify progression to the next step is blocked when required eligibility attributes are missing

  12. Verify displayed member information is not automatically refreshed when eligibility data changes after the form is rendered

Questions to BA

  1. Which eligibility attributes are considered required for progression and which are optional only for display?

  2. What exact blocking message should be shown when required eligibility attributes are missing?

  3. For missing optional fields, what is the expected UI behavior: hide field, show empty field, or hide whole section?

  4. For missing representative information, should the system hide the section or show an empty section?

  5. What exact validation rules apply to Treatment Start Date?

  6. What exact behavior is expected for Save & Exit if no case record has been created yet?

fields.System.Title: Testing 181259
fields.System.Description:
Testing of this story could not be started yet, because of the list on the reasons:
  1. BE and FE should agree in advance ebout the list of attributes.
  2. Deployment to the QA env should be performed.
Test cases:

 1. Invoke endpoint 'members/<id>/with-related-data' and check all the fields according to the Dictionary: https://oncologyanalytics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NewUM1/pages/5078581249/Member+Identification+and+Coverage#Must-be-one-of-the-Health-Plans-the-user-is-authorized-to-access-(payer-based-access-control).

2. Try to invoke the endpoint without uthentication.
3. Try to invoke the endpoint with empty id
4. Try to invoke id that is not available in the DB
5. Invoke endpoint with id not in UUID format

fields.System.Title: Create a stepper component based on design
fields.System.Description:
Stepper component should be shared across the app and placed inside a library.

It should be able to dynamically render steps ui.

Props to have:

orientation:horizontal | stacked (default is horizontal);
steps: Step[];

type Step {
id: string|number;
title: string;
subTitle:string;
isActive: boolean;
isDisabled: boolean;
isFilled: boolean;
}

fields.System.Description:
We should create a stepper CaseIntake container component that will contain all of the steps and keep the flow.


CaseIntake container should:
  • Be able to contain steps.
  • Should have it's own context that tracks the progress if filled, active, not filled steps
  • Should contain next / previous steps handlers that will be populated via context to the child components.

    p.s in the future it will handler the loading of steps configuration.

fields.System.Title: [BE] Define Case Submission Confirmation Response Contract (Prototype)
fields.System.Description:

  • confirmation status (success/failure)
  • case identifier (Case ID / Draft Case ID)

  • minimal timestamps / reference number if available
